title: Format of Overflow Tables
text: Format of Overflow Tables Uniface overflow tables are in a format that only Uniface can understand. If you use a non-Uniface product to access the data, the product must be configured to understand overflow tables. Overflow Table Names The overflow table or file has the same name as the main entity, preceded by an initial ‘O’. For example, the overflow file for the CORRESP entity, if stored in Sequential, has the name OCORRESP.udf . For this reason, do not create entities with names that differ only by the addition of an initial ‘O’. CAUTION: If overflow tables are created, remember to back these with the rest of your data. Record Length in Fixed-Length Record Databases The record length of the overflow file in DBMSs that only support fixed-length records is the sum of the length of the primary key fields plus 68, rounded up to the next multiple of 64, minus one. For example, if the primary key of the occurrence contains three fields each with a length of 25 bytes, the record length of the overflow file is 191 bytes. 3 * 25 = 75 75 + 68 = 143 Next multiple of 64 is 192 Record length is 191 Copy Variable-Length Fields in Fixed-Length Record Databases The variable-length portion of a variable-length field in fixed-length record DBMSs is added after the fixed-length fields. Uniface tries to compute the most efficient total record length. The record is extended by 32 bytes, then rounded up to the next multiple of 64 bytes, minus one. For example, if the sum of the lengths of the fixed-length fields is 50, the record length is 127 bytes. 50 + 32 = 82 Next multiple of 64 is 128 Record length is 127 Copy Related Links Overflow Tables DBMSs may impose upper limits to the number of fields in a table, the length of a field, and the length of a record. Some DBMSs do not support variable-length fields with a high maximum length. To overcome these limitations, Uniface provides an overflow mechanism, in which data that is represented as a single modeled entity is automatically split into two tables—the base table and an overflow table.
